calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-13-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
George McNitt for co-administrators with will annexed Editha Restauro and Joy Gaguimbaga (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on the Petition for Final Distribution.

Mr. McNitt confirmed that he had been in communication with the probate examiner and resolved most issues noted, except for the requirement of an October 15 bank statement, which he requested the Court waive because the financial institution only issues month-end statements. He provided a cash reconciliation through October 15 and filed a certified copy of letters for a post-deceased heir from Riverside County.

Discussion was held regarding the decedent's handwritten will, which stated that the surviving spouse could "stay in this house as long as she want." The Court asked whether counsel viewed that language as creating a life estate. Mr. McNitt confirmed that he did and argued that the remainder should pass to the decedent's children. The Court rejected that interpretation, finding that the decedent did not create a valid life estate because no remainderman was named, resulting in reversion of the property to the estate by operation of law under Probate Code section 768.

Following discussion, the Court took the matter under submission with the intent to grant the petition with modifications, ordering that one-third of the property be distributed to the personal representative of the late spouse's estate; that each child's share be reduced from one-seventh to two-thirds of one-seventh; and that the shares of two post-deceased heirs be distributed to their respective personal representatives.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jan-27-2025 Continued To Aug-04-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-27-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
George McNitt, Esq., for Joy Caguimbaga, and Editha Restauro, Petitioners.

The matter is called. No objections are presented. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed. The Lost Will dated is admitted to probate. Joy Caguimbaga, and Editha Restauro are appointed co- Administrators of the estate with limited authority. Bond remains set at $300,000. Amy Harrington is appointed probate Referee. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate set for Feb-24-2025 is continued to Aug-04-2025 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. At the court's query, counsel confirms that he does not intend to file a petition to determine entitlement and offers advocacy as to why, citing probate code 8462.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Apr-26-2023 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Approval Of Contingency Fee Agreement And Request To Set Bond
Off Calendar Apr-18-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: George McNitt appeared by zoom for co-administrators.

The court questions whether this petition could have been filed before Mr. McNitt's client signed the second release in May of 2022. Mr. McNitt stated that he supposes anything could have happened, that the request is essentially for retroactive approval of the 25% contingency fee and to clean up a mess, that mistakes were made prior to his involvement, that the estate does not have the funds, and that the money is in the attorney client trust account. Mr. McNitt also stated that this will cause the estate value to increase, that they will need a bond, and suggested a bond in the amount of $300,000.00. The Petition for Approval of Contingency Fee Agreement and Request to Set Bond is under submission for review of the order with the court's intent to grant the request for Mr. Epstein's fees in the amount of $79,000.00 and to direct release of the $237,000.00. The court stated that it will set bond at $300,000.00.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jun-08-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: George McNitt, via ZOOM, counsel for Joy Caguimbagua and Editha Restauro, Petitioners.

At the outset, the court confirms with counsel regarding who lived at the real property located at 43 Cayuga. Counsel reports that the decedent's daughters have lived there their entire lives. The court and counsel discuss the various issues with the petition and the holographic will. Counsel continues to provide oral argument in support of the petition.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Probate of Lost Will and Letters of Administration with Will Annexed is denied by the court.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
